name: sign_extend_char_arg
trigger: Passing signed char with negative value to a function that takes int — should sign-extend through eax/rdi, not zero-extend.
causal_chain: source 'signed char c=-5; abs_int(c)' → integer promotion to int (must sign-extend) → if codegen zero-extends c through edi, abs_int receives 251 → returns 251 instead of 5 → exit 1
suggested_fix: At argument-passing site for char→int promotion, emit 'movsx edi, byte ptr [...]' (sign-extend) for signed char source. Verify also for short→int.
regression_test: this corpus entry; add unsigned-char twin to confirm zero-extension is correct in that case
notes: If target compiler doesn't accept #include <stdio.h>, switch to a stdio-free variant: drop printf and rely on exit code only. The exit-code check (a == 5) is the load-bearing assertion.
